title: My body is allowed to fall
subtitle: An affirmation for when you need permission to let go of physical holding at bedtime.
excerpt: A short affirmation for bedtime, for when the physical act of releasing tension before sleep requires an explicit instruction to yourself. Say it once, slowly, in bed.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 3 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025), "Calm the body."

content.text: A short affirmation for bedtime. Some bodies need an explicit instruction to release before they will, one clear sentence of permission.
My body is allowed to fall. The day is done. I can let go now.
Say it once, slowly, in bed. Let the exhale after the last word be deliberate, the physical version of "letting go now." If one pass isn't enough, say it again.

Adapted from Day 3 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised by Rebecca J Page. Day 3 deals with the physical side of wired-at-night: the body that will not stand down even when you are exhausted.
